claim: Florida legally designated the Fenholloway an industrial (Class V) river
exact_statistic: 1947 statute; Class V = Navigation/Utility/Industrial Use, least stringent standard
note: Enacted to attract the pulp mill; restoration fight sought reclassification back toward Class III

claim: Mill built by Procter & Gamble; began operating
exact_statistic: Start of operations 1954 (69 years to closure)
note: Origin of the P&G -> Buckeye -> Georgia-Pacific chain

claim: Discharge volume into the Fenholloway
exact_statistic: Up to ~50 million gallons/day
note: Consistent with EPA NPDES effluent figures; authenticate DMRs as Tier1

claim: Waste contained dioxins (chlorine-bleaching byproduct)
exact_statistic: Dioxins present (known human carcinogen)
note: Presence documented; get EPA study as Tier1

claim: EPA study found river dioxin far above standard
exact_statistic: ~1,900x the agency standard
note: 1990 EPA study; obtain the actual study

claim: Endocrine disruption in river fish
exact_statistic: Female fish developing male sex characteristics
note: Well-known Fenholloway intersex-fish case

claim: Documented human-exposure pathway: fish-consumption advisory
exact_statistic: Largemouth bass advisory = DO NOT EAT (dioxin)
note: EPA had data Feb 1989; state delayed ~18 mo; Sept 1990 EPA urged warning; obtain FDOH advisory as Tier1

claim: Private drinking-water wells fouled
exact_statistic: Wells ran brown and smelly for years (residents blamed mill)
note: Alleged, not court-proven; see prior class action (X03)

claim: Federal NPDES discharge permit base number
exact_statistic: FL0000876 (exact current suffix FL0000876-IW1S-009-NR unconfirmed)
note: Base number confirmed; verify current application suffix via EPA/FDEP

claim: 1995 FDEP agreement required discharge relocation
exact_statistic: Process changes + relocate the discharge point in the river
note: Documented origin of the discharge-relocation saga; obtain the agreement as Tier1

claim: 2003: company chose pipeline over process cleanup
exact_statistic: Buckeye rejected oxygen-delignification/wetlands cleanup; insisted on Gulf pipeline
note: Relevant to notice/intent and standard of care

claim: Coastal pipeline (GP: 2013 -> Sept 2020)
exact_statistic: GP bought mill 2013; invested $300M+; Fenholloway Water Quality Project (~15-mi pipeline) completed Sept 2020
note: Upgrades earlier approximate dates; river later reported substantially cleaned up

claim: Outfall IDs / identity of the newer drain duct
exact_statistic: D-001 / D-002 (type unconfirmed: industrial vs stormwater vs remediation)
note: Read permit engineering drawings; essential unresolved distinction

claim: Georgia-Pacific announced the closure
exact_statistic: Announced Sept 18-20, 2023 (weeks after Hurricane Idalia Aug 30 2023)

claim: Mill ceased production
exact_statistic: ~Nov 17, 2023 (69 years)
note: Prior thread "ceased 2024" imprecise; production stopped late 2023

claim: Equipment air-gapped / decommissioned
exact_statistic: By Dec 31, 2024
note: Reconciles the "2024" wording in FDEP notice

claim: Title V air permit revoked
exact_statistic: April 2025 (unconfirmed); permit 1230001-127-AV issued Apr 10 2024
note: Revocation date not confirmed; pull FDEP air permit record 1230001

claim: Wastewater treatment + groundwater monitoring remain active after closure
exact_statistic: Primary/secondary/tertiary treatment, reuse, stormwater, GW monitoring
note: Subject of 2026 FDEP permit process; ask why a non-manufacturing site still needs it

claim: GP prohibited from using the coastal pipeline
exact_statistic: Lost pipeline use after legal/easement dispute
note: Drives the request to discharge back upstream

claim: FDEP draft permit to resume up-river discharge
exact_statistic: Up to 12 million gallons/day at original mill-site location
note: Treated wastewater/stormwater back into the Fenholloway

claim: FDEP added monitoring after public comment
exact_statistic: More frequent discharge monitoring; additional GW wells
note: FDEP response to comments; public meeting held

claim: Union County ranks #1 in Florida for lung incidence
exact_statistic: Highest FL county rate (large incarcerated-population confounder)
note: Taylor is NOT the US high; prior thread retracted that

claim: Taylor County lung & bronchus incidence, rank 2 in Florida
exact_statistic: 65.0 per 100,000 -- appears as LATE-STAGE, not all-stages
note: Comparators FL/US 53.3 are all-stages; late-vs-all-stages mismatch overstates excess; re-pull all-stages

claim: Female lung & bronchus incidence elevated
note: Also surfaced with late-stage context; re-pull sex-specific all-stages

claim: Cancer registry has a built-in reporting lag
exact_statistic: Complete diagnosis-year file ~2 years after year-end; reporting within ~6 months
note: Do not fabricate a 2026 rate; use newest complete year and label coverage

claim: Young girls getting breast cancer cluster
exact_statistic: Not measurable from public county rates
note: Small-county suppression (counts 1-9 not published); needs FCDS case-level request with defined age bands

claim: Confounder: county is very small
exact_statistic: Taylor County population ~21,796 (2020 Census)
note: Drives rate instability and subgroup suppression; cuts both ways in litigation

claim: Confounder: smoking drives lung cancer
exact_statistic: Florida adult smoking ~11.3% (2022); Taylor County rate must be pulled
note: Rural lung excess often smoking-driven; must adjust before environmental attribution

claim: No known formal cancer-cluster investigation for Taylor County
exact_statistic: None found in search; FDOH Cancer Concerns process exists to request one
note: Filing a request creates an official record and can compel a state review

claim: Perry water source
exact_statistic: Three wells, Floridan Aquifer
note: Reported in CCR; obtain CCR + source-well data as Tier1

claim: Perry TTHM drinking-water violation
exact_statistic: In violation of TTHM standard, Q1 2024, at 700 Charles Hendry Road
note: EPA/EWG flag serious violation for the period

claim: Perry exact contaminant levels
exact_statistic: TTHM 83.01 ppb (MCL 80); HAA5 49.64 (MCL 60); Mn 0.35 ppm (MCL 0.05); As 0.63 ppb; Cr 0.59 ppb
note: Consistent with a real CCR but exact decimals not fetched here; pull the CCR PDF

claim: Do the TTHMs implicate the mill?
exact_statistic: No - TTHMs are chlorination disinfection byproducts
note: Water-treatment issue, not pulp-mill effluent; no mill-to-well pathway shown; Mn also natural in aquifer

claim: Did the mill cause Taylor County cancer burden?
exact_statistic: Not demonstrated
note: Exposure chain source->pathway->dose->cancer not shown; needs expert opinion

claim: Did mill contaminants reach Perry drinking water?
exact_statistic: Not demonstrated
note: mill->groundwater->well->population pathway unproven; specific research target

claim: Prior federal class action over well water was dismissed
exact_statistic: 100+ residents; dismissed for failure to prove sufficient financial damage
note: MOST IMPORTANT strategic fact; pull the docket/opinion/citation as Tier1; cure damages + causation

claim: Operator reportedly resisted EPA testing
exact_statistic: Pulp mill polluter blocks EPA test results (2000)
note: Potential spoliation/concealment theme; authenticate
